page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1.8xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.d1 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 32 vCPUs. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on a KVM hypervisor.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, which consists of 94489 GB of HDD storage. This high-density local storage is bundled directly with the instance, making it suitable for workloads requiring substantial raw storage capacity. For connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present on this hardware configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.d1.8xlarge offers a high volume of local HDD storage per unit of compute, presenting a specialized resource density profile for legacy data processing. Given the reliance on HDD media rather than SSDs, this instance is optimized for sequential read and write operations rather than random IOPS-heavy workloads. It is best suited for large-scale data warehousing, MapReduce jobs, log processing, and distributed file systems like HDFS where massive storage volume is prioritized over low-latency disk access.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d1.8xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance configured with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 vCPUs operating at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The primary differentiator of this KVM-based instance is its massive bundled local storage, offering 94489 GB of HDD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps, and there are no GPU accelerators included. This hardware profile is designed for high-capacity sequential data processing rather than high-IOPS workloads. It is highly suited for distributed file systems, large-scale log processing, data warehousing, and MapReduce workloads that require substantial local storage volume alongside dedicated compute resources.
